summary: Tail-recursive prefix-balance scan over an i64 slice (below_zero_at) with a running i64 accumulator; iff-decomposed soundness/completeness with an existential prefix-sum witness — the strong-induction invariant is strengthened with a Lean-side prefix_sum_int spec tying balance.toInt to the partial Int sum, soundness derives ¬Int64.addOverflow by contradiction from the function returning RustM.ok true (unfolding the do-block and casing on the fail bind), and completeness is gated on an explicit "every prefix sum fits in i64" hfit precondition that rules out overflow via Int64.addOverflow_iff; three step lemmas (oob/found/recurse) close each branch with one unfold + simp.
